title: Motorola Edge 70 Fusion full specifications revealed ahead of launch: here's what to expect
summary: Motorola has unveiled the Edge 70 Fusion at MWC in Barcelona. The devices features a 6.8-inch AMOLED display, Snapdragon 7s Gen 4 processor, 7,000mAh battery, and dual rear cameras.

title: AI+ Pulse 2 with 120Hz display and 6,000mAh battery launched in India, price starts at ₹5,999
summary: AI+ has unveiled the Pulse 2 smartphone featuring an octa-core Unisoc T7250 processor, up to 6GB RAM, and a 6,000mAh battery. It runs on NxtQuantum OS based on Android 16 and will be available starting March 11 for ₹5,999.

title: March smartphone launches: Xiaomi 17 series, Nothing Phone 4a, iPhone 17e and more
summary: February saw numerous smartphone launches, including Samsung's S26 lineup and iQOO 15R. March promises even more launches with the iPhone 17e, Xiaomi 17 series, Nothing Phone 4a series, and more.&nbsp;

title: Amid ‘Cancel ChatGPT’ trend, Anthropic launches feature to help users switch to Claude
summary: Anthropic has launched a new import memory feature for Claude, which allows paid users to bring memories from other AI chatbots. The feature comes at a time when Anthropic has seen strong public sentiment in its favour following the cancellation of its deal with the US government.

title: Apple's ‘big week’ announcements begin from today: iPhone 17e, new iPads, budget MacBook and everything expected
summary: Apple plans to unveil several new products this week, including the iPhone 17e, new MacBooks, and iPads. CEO Tim Cook has hinted at a multi-day launch starting 2 March, which could conclude with the event on 4 March.

title: Nothing Phone 4a and 4a Pro launching on 5 March: Design, expected specs and more
summary: Nothing is set to launch its Phone 4 (a) series on 5 March. The launch event is also likely to see the unveling of new Headphone (a) with bold colors and long battery life. The Phone 4a will likely include a Snapdragon 7s Gen 4 processor, a triple camera setup along with a 6.78 inch OLED display.

title: OpenAI defends rival Anthropic against Pentagon ban, Sam Altman calls it ‘extremely scary precedent’
summary: OpenAI CEO Sam Altman expressed concerns over the US government's ban on rival Anthropic, labeling it a scary precedent. Despite OpenAI's past tensions with Anthropic, Altman defended the rival AI lab during a recent AMA session.

title: Did Grok predict US attack on Iran? Elon Musk claims victory for xAI after viral test
summary: &nbsp;Elon Musk's Grok chatbot uniquely predicted the date of the US strike on Iran as 28 February 2026, even prior to the attack. The prediction was made by the AI chatbot in a methodological test on 25 February.

summary: Dario Amodei has called the US government’s decision to label Anthropic a supply chain risk “retaliatory and punitive”, saying it is unprecedented for an American company.
